description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, giving it a ribbed or slatted appearance. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the mottled, grayish stone surface between them. The block is solid and robust, with clean, straight edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing its geometric simplicity. The coloration is predominantly dark gray with subtle variations, suggesting a natural stone material. This type of block is commonly used as a drainage cover, urban barrier, or decorative paving element in city environments.
description: A rectangular, grooved stone block with a ribbed top surface and smooth, dark gray sides. The grooves create a striking striped pattern across the top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's thickness clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal grooves and solid structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the groove depth and the block's rectangular profile.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage area
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no moss or chipping.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or stonework
